meaning: n.慌乱,狼狈,混乱,激动vt.使酩醉,使慌张,使激动vi.慌慌张张的行动,混乱
examples: The honk of horn fluster the driver.
Accept the fluster of lost door keys.
She was a very calm person. Nothing could fluster her.
But the problem--publishers who inflate pay-per-click ad fees with automatic clicking software--doesn't fluster Ghosemajumder or other Googlers.
Well, at first, there may be a fluster in the trees or a violent shake in the canopy above your head.
